# Sok Thy — The Spider

**សុខ ធី** is Cambodia's breakout MMA talent, blending a Kun Khmer striking base with Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu submissions. Known for his unorthodox movement and creative striking angles, Sok Thy has become one of the most exciting fighters on the FNF roster.

## Fighting Style

Thy is a rangey counter-striker who uses his length to pick opponents apart from the outside. When the fight hits the ground, his guard game is among the best in Southeast Asian MMA — he holds a brown belt in BJJ under the tutelage of a Gracie Barra affiliate in Siem Reap.

## Career Highlights

- **FNF Lightweight #1 Contender**
- Submission of the Night at FNF: Angkor Rising II (rear-naked choke, R2)
- **7 submission victories** in 18 career wins
- Former Kun Khmer regional champion before transitioning to MMA

## Training

Thy splits his training between Angkor Fight Club in Siem Reap (striking/MMA) and Gracie Barra Cambodia (BJJ). He is known for his analytical approach to fight preparation, studying hours of opponent footage before each bout.

# Prom Samnang — The Golden Elbow

**ប្រុម សំណាង** is one of Cambodia's most decorated Kun Khmer fighters, known for his devastating elbow strikes and relentless clinch work. Fighting out of Phnom Penh's legendary Sovannaphum Gym, Samnang has been a fixture of the Friday Night Fights card since its inaugural season.

## Fighting Style

Samnang is a pressure fighter who excels in the clinch. His signature technique — a spinning elbow from the clinch break — has earned him the nickname *"The Golden Elbow."* He combines traditional Kun Khmer footwork with explosive knee strikes, making him dangerous at every range.

## Career Highlights

- **FNF Welterweight Champion** (2024–Present)
- **12-fight win streak** spanning two seasons
- Featured on the main card of FNF: Kingdom Warriors III
- Represented Cambodia at the Southeast Asian Muay Thai Championship

## Training

Samnang trains six days a week at Sovannaphum Gym in Phnom Penh, under the guidance of Kru Vicheka. His regimen includes morning roadwork along the Mekong, technical sparring, and heavy bag sessions focused on elbow combinations.
